records.ety: [Pref. di- (= L. dis-) + minish: cf. L. diminuere, F. diminuer, OE. diminuen. See Dis-, and Minish.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Diminished (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Diminishing.]

defs: To make smaller in any manner; to reduce in bulk or amount; to lessen; -- opposed to augment or increase.
quotes: Not diminish, but rather increase, the debt.

defs: To lessen the authority or dignity of; to put down; to degrade; to abase; to weaken.
quotes: This doth nothing diminish their opinion.
I will diminish them, that they shall no more rule over the nations.
O thou . . . at whose sight all the stars Hide their diminished heads.

defs: To make smaller by a half step; to make (an interval) less than minor; as, a diminished seventh.

quotes: Neither shall ye diminish aught from it.

defs: To become or appear less or smaller; to lessen; as, the apparent size of an object diminishes as we recede from it.
